name: natvent_exit_hvac_shutoff
description: Issue #337: When a door/window is open (_paused_by_door=True) and a hot-day classification cycle fires, apply_classification must suppress the comfort band and keep HVAC off — not re-arm cool mode through open windows.
notes: Occupant outcome: User left windows open on a hot morning. CA paused the AC when the window opened. On the next 30-min classification cycle, CA must NOT turn AC back on through open windows — that would waste energy and push unconditioned outdoor air through the home.

Scenario effectiveness: If the _paused_by_door guard is deleted from apply_classification(), the classification event would emit 'classification_applied' and call _set_hvac_mode('cool'). The second assertion would fail because the last decision at 08:30 would be 'classification_applied' instead of 'classification_suppressed_paused'. The scenario WILL fail without the guard.

Two-way alignment: production apply_classification() checks _paused_by_door before applying any band. If thermostat is already off it emits classification_suppressed_paused and returns. This scenario exercises exactly that code path.

Setup: thermostat must be in a non-off mode before the sensor opens, otherwise handle_door_window_open() does not set _paused_by_door (the guard requires pre_pause_mode != 'off'). We start in cool mode (initial_thermostat_mode=cool) on a hot day.

Harness note: 'classification_suppressed_paused' is mapped in outcomes.py _map_event_to_outcome() to the canonical decision 'classification_suppressed_paused' (Issue #337 closed this gap). The expect value below matches that canonical string.

events.note: Hot morning: outdoor 78F already warm; AC was running (initial_thermostat_mode=cool) keeping indoor at 72F.
User opens window. Thermostat is in cool mode — handle_door_window_open detects pre_pause_mode='cool' != 'off', sets _paused_by_door=True, turns HVAC off, emits sensor_opened result=paused.

30-min classification cycle fires on a hot day, requesting hvac_mode=cool. apply_classification must detect _paused_by_door=True, keep HVAC off, and emit classification_suppressed_paused — NOT classification_applied.

assertions.reason: Window opened while AC was running — handle_door_window_open sets _paused_by_door=True, emits sensor_opened result=paused, and turns HVAC off. This establishes the paused state the #337 guard depends on.

apply_classification fires on a hot day requesting cool mode, but _paused_by_door=True. The guard suppresses the band, confirms HVAC stays off, and emits classification_suppressed_paused. outcomes.py maps this event to the canonical 'classification_suppressed_paused' decision (Issue #337). Without the #337 guard, apply_classification would emit 'classification_applied' instead — making this assertion fail.

verdict.summary: apply_classification respects _paused_by_door — suppresses hot-day cool band when window is open (Issue #337)
verdict.observed_behavior: sensor_open → paused (HVAC off, _paused_by_door=True) → classification hot/cool → classification_suppressed_paused (HVAC stays off)
verdict.expected_behavior: Classification cycle on a hot day does NOT re-arm AC while windows are open — occupant avoids AC running against open windows
